items.text: FALL CAMPUS PHOTO CONTEST — Submit photos by November 15. Theme: 'Autumn at Maple Hall.' $5 entry per photo. Drop submissions at the Arts Office, Room 204. Winner receives $200 and a feature in the campus magazine. Open to all current students. Bring your ID.

questions.stem: According to the flyer, what is the entry fee per photo?
questions.explanation: The flyer states '$5 entry per photo.'

questions.stem: According to the flyer, where should photos be submitted?

options.A: At the campus magazine office on First Street.
options.B: At the arts office website portal page.
options.C: At a contest committee desk in Main Hall.
options.D: At Room 204 of the Arts Office.

questions.explanation: The flyer says 'Drop submissions at the Arts Office, Room 204.'

items.text: NOTICE: Water Fountain Maintenance. The water fountains in Henderson Hall will be out of service from October 4 through October 6 for routine cleaning. Please use the fountains in adjacent Bryant Hall during this period. Maintenance staff will be on site each weekday from 8 AM to 4 PM.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what is the reason for the closure?

options.C: Installation of a new water filter.
options.D: A scheduled inspection by city officials.

questions.explanation: The notice states the closure is 'for routine cleaning.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred about students who need water during this period?

options.A: They should buy bottled water at the bookstore.
options.B: They should walk to a nearby building's fountains.
options.C: They must wait until the maintenance crew finishes.
options.D: They will not be allowed to leave Henderson Hall.

questions.explanation: The notice directs students to 'use the fountains in adjacent Bryant Hall.'

items.text:
Hi Sarah,

Just confirming our calculus tutoring session this Thursday, March 12 at 3:00 PM. We'll meet in the Math Help Center, Room 110 of Wexler Hall. Bring your problem set from chapter 7. Let me know if anything changes.

Thanks,
Daniel

questions.stem: According to the email, where is the tutoring session?

options.C: At the campus library learning center.

questions.explanation: The email says they will meet 'in the Math Help Center, Room 110 of Wexler Hall.'

questions.stem: The word 'confirming' in the email is closest in meaning to
questions.explanation: Daniel is checking that the appointment is still on, which is verifying it.

items.text: SPRING PHOTO CONTEST — 'Cherry Blossoms on Campus.' Deadline: April 20. $3 entry fee per photo, payable at submission. Drop entries at the Student Union welcome desk between 10 AM and 4 PM. First prize: $150 plus a one-year subscription to the campus art magazine. Open to all undergraduates.

questions.stem: According to the flyer, what is the first prize?
questions.explanation: The flyer states 'First prize: $150 plus a one-year subscription.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred about students who want to enter?

options.A: They must drop entries off during set hours.
options.B: They can submit entries by email or mail.
options.C: They will be charged after winning the contest.
options.D: They are required to attend a spring festival.

questions.explanation: The flyer specifies that entries are dropped at a welcome desk between 10 AM and 4 PM, implying in-person submission within set hours.
